1.title: Challenge Red-01: AD Scan Discovery
1.desc: The adversary has launched an initial scan targeting the Domain Controller. Check the threat warning or network details. Identify the target IP address of the Domain Controller to establish connection details.
1.hint: Look at the Attacker Terminal's header line '[+] TARGET SCOPE: 192.168.20.100' or check the live logs.

2.title: Challenge Red-02: AD Database Encryption
2.desc: The attacker has pivoted into the AD network and triggered a ransomware macro script. Identify the target Active Directory database file name being encrypted.
2.hint: Look at the attacker script or exploit description. It is the core Active Directory database file name (e.g. NTDS.dit).

3.title: Challenge Blue-03: VLAN Isolation & Defense
3.desc: A critical domain controller breach is active! Quarantine the compromised server subnet. Find the VLAN identifier that has been isolated to contain the threat.
3.hint: Look at the terminal logs or check the telemetry grid's quarantined network link. The isolated VLAN is labeled starting with VLAN_.

1.title: Challenge Red-04: SQL Scanner Discovery
1.desc: The attacker is scanning the web services interface of the E-Commerce portal. Identify the scanning tool name used to discover SQL injection flaws.
1.hint: Review the live SIEM event logs or the attacker terminal scan. It is a well-known command-line database injection tool.

2.title: Challenge Red-05: SQLi Target Endpoint
2.desc: The attacker found a SQL injection vulnerability. They are sending unauthenticated queries to harvest user records. Identify the vulnerable endpoint path being targeted.
2.hint: Look at the attacker command `/api/v1/users` or the live web server access logs.

3.title: Challenge Blue-06: WAF Response Action
3.desc: The SQL database breach has been detected by security tools. Incident response triggered a WAF blocking rule. What is the firewall status keyword applied to the attacker session?
3.hint: Check the terminal feedback in Phase 3 or the SIEM dashboard status indicators. It is in all caps: BLOCKED.

1.title: Challenge Red-07: MS HTML Vulnerability Scan
1.desc: The attacker sent a malicious phishing attachment exploiting a Microsoft MSHTML remote code execution vulnerability. Identify the CVE identifier of this exploit.

2.title: Challenge Red-08: Phishing Payload Extension
2.desc: An employee opened the phishing document, launching a macro payload. What is the extension appended to the encrypted accounting document copies?
2.hint: Check the alerts in Panel 3 or the attacker CLI feedback. Look for the file extension name starting with a dot.

3.title: Challenge Blue-09: Account Revocation & Freeze
3.desc: The security team is containing the mail session compromise. What is the active directory credential status applied to the executive session?
3.hint: Check the restoration logs or status indicators in Phase 3. It is in all caps: REVOKED.
